mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held at low, stable levels reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, providing a persistent tailwind for S&P 500 returns regardless of short-term news flow. This is an always-on trend effect that strengthens as FEDFUNDS remains below its trailing 12-month average.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.79% as of t-1mo, near cycle lows
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level (inverted) and forward 20d return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or returns during low-rate periods are not significantly higher than the full-sample mean (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above a critical threshold signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede consumer retrenchment, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week as markets price in weaker earnings expectations.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 5.5% or increases > 0.2pp month-over-month
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE crosses the 5.5% threshold, subsequent 5-day SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the effect is not statistically distinguishable from random walk (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Joint weakness in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) reflects a synchronized contraction in consumer and residential investment demand that is not captured by either series alone. When both decline together over a 2-month window, forward 20-day S&P 500 returns turn negative as aggregate demand deceleration broadens.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 2-month change < 0 AND HOUST 2-month change < 0 simultaneously
failure_condition: Joint decline episodes in RSAFS and HOUST are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or the interaction term's marginal predictive contribution over either series alone is not significant (delta Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: During an early-cycle post-recession recovery, sustained acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) signals rebuilding supply-side capacity and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon. This effect is regime-specific and does not hold in late-cycle or contraction phases.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=118.527 at t-2mo, GDPC1=11078.2 at t-4mo
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO growth and forward 60-day returns is below 0.03 during periods classified as early-cycle recovery, or the effect direction reverses (negative correlation) in more than one out of three historical early-cycle episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above a tightening threshold following a period of near-zero rates, markets interpret this as the onset of a hiking cycle, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases > 0.5pp within 3 months from a prior low base
failure_condition: Following triggered rate increases, SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or no significant rank correlation (Rank IC < 0.03) is observed across rolling 3-year windows during tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects continuous labor market expansion that supports consumer income and spending, generating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: The rolling correlation between PAYEMS month-over-month change and forward 20-day SPX return falls below 0.03 Rank IC over any 2-year window, indicating no persistent trend relationship.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) above a threshold signals stronger-than-expected consumer demand, prompting a short-term rally in S&P 500 prices over the following week as analysts revise near-term earnings estimates upward.
failure_condition: Instances where RSAFS growth exceeds the 0.8% threshold are followed by negative or flat 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or the effect shows no statistical significance (p>0.10) across the sample.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) and a low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) creates a negative real interest rate environment that initially boosts risk asset demand, but once inflation growth outpaces rate normalization expectations, forward S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon turn negative as inflation risk premia rise. The effect is conditional on both series moving in tandem, not either alone.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 2.5% while FEDFUNDS remains below 1%
regime_hint: negative real rate / inflation acceleration regime
failure_condition: During periods meeting the joint CPIAUCSL/FEDFUNDS trigger, forward 60-day SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term does not improve Rank IC by more than 0.02 relative to either series used independently.
